Title: SDS-PAGE using Biorad System
Author: Suyog Basnet
Change Reason: Updated file location throughout the document to avoid having full path for file location.   Pt. 3: Added responsibilities for Verifier Pt. 5: Removed reference to SOP   Pt. 6: Updated that the specified reagents are only an examples. This allows changing the reagents, if necessary,  without updating SOP.  Pt. 6: Deleted pt. For IgM analysis: Known IgG sample is used as control sample".   Pt. 8.2 Sample dilution refered in pt. 9.3. The dilution is described after preparing loading buffer.  Updated "Pt. 8.3 Always use a QA approved IgG sample as control together with IgM sample". It is not necessary to take an old IgM batch as control. (Ref CAPA ID_35)   Pt.9.4.2: Removed "and rinse the wells thoroughly with Milli RO water or running buffer" as this is defined in pt.9.4.6, where it is more practical.     Updated Pt. 9.8 Sentence rewritten.    Pt. 9.9.1: Added "Optional: Empthy lanes in the gel can be cropped out as mentioned above in Pt. 9.8.12 and Pt. 9.8.13 before analysis.  Pt. 9.9.4 Updated "Use the tool “Adjust” to manually fit the grid to the lanes in the picture. Adjust the grid to include all the bands in the standard lane. Adjust the grid between:    Top of the gel: About mid-point between sample application well and the fist band of standard.     Bottom of the gel: After the last band of standard, above the dye front.  See Figure 3 as an example." Pt. 9.9.7 Updated "Check that all bands are detected. Sometimes some impurities can be detected as protein band. Refer  Pt.9.9.8 Added information for labelling wells with sample ID. Removed "Pt.9.9.10 Save the analysis file (SCN file): F:Lab\BioRad Stainfree gels\SDSxxx" as this is already described in pt. 9.9.8   Removed "Pt. 10.1.4 The most intense band of the standard should be less intense than the most intense band of the sample" according to CAPA_111 and after discussion with technical support personnel from BioRad.  Merged Pt. 10.1.1 and pt 10.1.2: 10 bands must be detected for the standard" with Pt 10.1.1 (Ref. CAPA_111) Added Pt. 10.1.2: Broken gels are approved if it does not interfere the lanes with samples and standard. Added Pt. 10.1.3 At least one of the samples prepared should have migrated into the gel. Always load a known IgG sample together with IgM samples.  Added pt. 10: If any of these specifications is not fulfilled, a non-conformance must be registered. the Gel must be re-run after the non-conformance has been approved. Updated pt. 10.2  Product approval with validation criteria and acceptance criteria     10.2.1 Validation criteria   Duplicates of test sample must be comparable    If this criteria specifications is not fulfilled, a non-conformance must be registered and approved, and the failed         sample must be re-run.      10.2.2 Acceptance criteria    To approve a product for a final release, the run must fulfill the following specifications:    The result of the product sample must meet the requirement of Product Specifications Empty lanes can be excluded from the analysis.  The analysis area of the gel should include all the bands of the protein standard.  If any of these specifications are not fulfilled, an Out-of-Specification report must be written and approved, and failed sample must be re-run. It is not necessary to re-run all the samples in the gel. Only the batch that failed can be re-run.  Updated Pt. 10.3: Specified that by two operators should independently verify that the gel meets technical and product approval requirements.  (ref. CAPA 85)    Added section: Pt. 11 Troubleshooting".      1. After protein bands are detected, some external impurities on the gel like dirt or bubbles, which obviously is not a protein band can interfere on the result and detected as a protein band by the sample. This impacts the purity of the sample. If the band is an artifact or any external impurities, the false band can be removed during analysis. After the false band is detected, select “-Delete” on the menu. Click on the false band to delete it. Added figure 4.        2. Artifacts are quite common during SDS-PAGE and could be misleading as impurities. The artifacts can be disregarded if they are weak bands and are not detected as band by the software (Figure 5). Added figure 5". This is added based on published literatures for eg. Liu H et. al., 2007"

Title: Human Resources
Author: Johanna Lindskog Frydendal
Change Reason: Complete review of document, updated to clearer define the purpose of the SOP, how Curida Diatec ensures appropriate levels of qualified and competent staff to provide the effective implementation of the QMS, to produce high quality products, meet customer needs and ensure a high level of customer satisfaction. Updated section for Definitions and Responsibilities to include new roles and responsibilities according to CMID 68. Added more clear responsibilities for each role in obtaining and developing competent employees.   Pt. 4.1: Updated with more information of the hiring process, including routine of evaluation internal candidates prior to external candidates for open positions. Pt. 4.2: Updated with explanation of archiving employee documentation in the HRM system, and information of restricted access according to GDPR regulations and Curida Diatec guidelines for Privacy. Ref: CMID 73 Implementation of HRM system. Signed original documents such as employee contracts, job descriptions and other relevant original documents are still archived in the locked fireproof archive cabinet. Only HR has access to this cabinet. Pt. 4.4: Added description of how to handle the original signed Job description. Pt. 4.5: Removed the Doc 5015.5 Competence mapping. This is now performed in the HRM system. Added description of overview of completed training in eQMS Bizzmine. Pt. 4.6: Added description of the purpose and procedure of how to perform the Performance Appraisal Dialogue in the HRM system, ref. CMID 73   Sections removed from this SOP: Comunication – it will be moved to SOP 5016 Management Responsibilities Meetings are held on a regular basis to ensure that relevant information is shared across the organisation. The frequency of meetings of each department is to be decided and facilitated by the Immediate Managers. Information from the administration to be decided and facilitated by the leader team. The agenda is decided by the facilitator of each meeting.    Contact with media – will be moved to SOP 5016 Management Responsibilities Only CEO and/or Head of Business Development is to comment to press regarding Curida Diatec activities.   Electronic signature statemnt– will be moved to SOP 5049 IT – Acceptable use Policy Username and Password are given to dedicated operators of instruments operated by integrated software at Curida Diatec. The username and Password must be unique to the person using it. The audit trail of the instrument must display a record of the operator who has performed the action. Operator must keep the password safe to avoid record and signature falsification. Operator is accountable for actions initiated under their electronic signature. Operator is responsible for reporting to the supervisor if there is suspicion of error. An electronic Signature Statement must be signed by all operator using Doc5015.9. The document will be prepared by HR, signed and filled in the personal binder by the operator.   Signature list– will be moved to SOP5028 Good Documentation Practice All employees who sign documentation must have defined signatures in the Log Book Signature List. An initial signature with three letters and a full signature will be assigned by QA. The Signature List is updated annually, and it is the responsibility of QA to make the list available, ensure that everyone signs and keep the Signature List in a locked cabinet (preferably fireproof cabinet).    Removed section Safety.

Title: Supplier Complaints
Author: Anniken Liljeberg
Change Reason: SOP is re-written in accordance with CMID 77   Procedure:Supplier complaint are documented in the BizzMine collection Supplier complaints. Details for completing the form is described in WIN5036_1. All supplier complaints must originate from one or more Non-conformance(s). All investigations shall be performed in the NC. If the investigation concludes that the fault is with the supplier, all corrective actions and root-cause assessments shall be performed by the supplier. QA will approve the supplier corrective action plan, action execution, and effectiveness check.Description of when a supplier corrective action shall be requestedDescription on how to proceed if a supplier does not perform actions in accordance with our claims and requirements, or if the corrective action taken proves to not be effective.Definitions of quality impact (classification) and reoccurrence aligned with the SOP5034 Non-conformance.   Change in responsibilities: Registration of a new supplier complaint can be done by all employees.The caseworker can, in addition to logistics team, be anyone with expert knowledge of or have a direct relationship with the supplied material/service.Department manager or SME is responsible for technical verification of the complaint and supplier response.QA is responsible for approval of the supplier corrective action plan, execution and effectiveness check.
